title: Publish-path resilience bundle: (A) decider Git Data API fallback when push 403s + (B) workspace-sync regen-and-push docs/index.html when data files are newer (mtime backstop)
related_props: HNOTE-OPERATOR-UNIVERSAL-PUSHER-001 (2026-04-26) — sibling rescue design that solved data-file rescue but did NOT cover generated artifacts. A and B are the missing piece for generated HTML.

PROP-049 (clone-depth standardization, 2026-05-20) — touches workspace-sync.md L581 in the strand-rescue recipe; PROP-050 inserts Design B at L491-493 inside Step 3. NO conflict — different anchors, different surfaces.

PROP-013 (NEVER_PUSH strand rescue, in workspace-sync.md Step 3.5) — Design B operates BEFORE Step 3.5, in Step 3, so any drift caught by Design B is staged in the same commit as the Step 4 universal push.
PROP-016 Mech A (stranded-patches sentinels, 2026-05-02) — Design A is forward-looking; existing stranded-patches files are NOT retroactively pushed (see post_patch_behavior).
related_docs: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (lines 39-91 NEVER_PUSH, 471-491 existing data-staged regen, 772-783 Step 4 commit/push)
scope_disclaimer: Proposal only. Tinker does NOT self-apply per operator preference in DIRECTIVE-20260520-001.scope_authority. Operator + cowork-claude apply after PROP review. No shadow-mode rollout requested — the 2026-05-20T05:31Z manual commit (7126e0fd) already proves the Git Data API mechanism works with the same PAT.
executive_summary: Decider's `node build.js publish` fails with HTTP 403 on `git push` whenever it runs in a session bound to the Devilwench identity (chronic ~1 month; PAT rotation off the table per operator). The universal-pusher rescue (added 2026-04-26) recovers DATA files via workspace-sync but leaves docs/index.html stale because workspace-sync's NEVER_PUSH deny-list excludes build artifacts. Result: 2+ day HTML drift incidents (today's WIN-070 caption math, ISS-2095 in 2026-05-17 window — at least 3rd recurrence). Design A (Git Data API fallback in build.js + decider self-apply) is the primary durable fix — the same PAT that 403s on `git push` succeeds on the REST API path (proven 2026-05-20 commit 7126e0fd via cowork-claude). Design B (workspace-sync regenerates docs/index.html when FUSE data files are newer than docs/index.html) is a complementary mtime-based backstop that catches any drift A doesn't cover and self-clears once A is operating. Together they remove the chronic drift surface without touching the underlying auth gap.

push_sites_identified.site: publish branch — `run('git push origin main', 'Push to GitHub')` on line 391, inside `if (committed) { ... }`
push_sites_identified.notes: PRIMARY patch target for Design A. The `run()` helper calls `execSync(cmd, { stdio: 'inherit' })` and `process.exit(1)` on failure; replacing with a wrapper that tries API fallback before exiting is the minimal-surface change.

push_sites_identified.site: decider self-apply push — `PUSH_OUT=$(git push origin main 2>&1)` on line 384, then conditional rescue block lines 388-435
push_sites_identified.purpose: decider self-apply patches push (separate from build.js publish; runs after apply-patches.js + tests pass)
push_sites_identified.notes: SECONDARY patch target for Design A. Highest-traffic push path (every decider run that has patches; hourly+). Wiring API fallback BETWEEN the git push and the universal-pusher rescue (set PUSH_RC=0 on API success) keeps the existing rescue branch intact as a third tier.

push_sites_identified.site: workspace-sync push sites — Step 3.5 strand rescue push (553), Step 4 main commit push (782), Step 4b run-report push (857)
push_sites_identified.notes: NOT a patch target for Design A. workspace-sync's identity has empirically pushed successfully throughout the decider 403 streak (per workspace-sync.md operational notes: "32+ push-failures over 5 days, 100% workspace-sync push success"). Design B adds a regen step BEFORE these push sites — no change to the push mechanism itself.

incident_today_detail.symptom: Live docs/index.html line 382 deployed text: "72 catalog entries = 69 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims" (69+2=71, off by one — missing WIN-070).
incident_today_detail.generator_state: build-scripts/generate-html.js line 500 already had the corrected template "${total} = 69 + 1 (WIN-070) + 2 collisions". Generator was correct; deployed HTML was 2+ days stale.
incident_today_detail.git_log_signature: 48h+ window of "Workspace sync: ..." commits, no successful `build.js publish` commit.
incident_today_detail.manual_fix: cowork-claude regenerated docs/index.html locally via /tmp invocation of generate-html.js, pushed via Git Data API (blob → tree → commit → update-ref). Commit 7126e0fd (2026-05-20T05:31Z). Same PAT that 403s on `git push` succeeded on every API call.
incident_today_detail.prior_recurrence: ISS-2095 (filed + closed 2026-05-18) — same symptom in 2026-05-16/17 window. At minimum 3rd recurrence.

pat_identity_analysis.finding: decider AND workspace-sync extract PAT from `git -C "${WORKSPACE}" remote get-url origin` (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md line uses inherited remote URL; workspace-sync.md line 19-20 explicitly does this). SAME PAT.
pat_identity_analysis.implication: workspace-sync's 100% push success rate during the decider 403 streak is NOT due to a different PAT — it is per-session/IP. Different scheduled-task sessions get different IPs, and the 403 is empirically per-session, per-IP. The PAT itself has contents:write (verified 2026-04-25, HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001).
pat_identity_analysis.design_consequence: Design A works because it bypasses git's HTTPS push code path entirely — the REST API uses HTTPS but a different code path that does not trip the per-session/IP restriction. Design B works because workspace-sync's git push has empirically been working — that asymmetry is preserved by NOT touching workspace-sync's push mechanism.

phase_1_design.label: Design A — Git Data API fallback for build.js publish AND decider self-apply
phase_1_design.rationale: The Git Data API uses 6 REST calls (blobs → ref → commit → tree → commit → refs/PATCH) to commit and update HEAD atomically. It bypasses git's HTTPS push protocol entirely, which is where the per-session/IP 403 originates. Today's manual test (commit 7126e0fd) confirms the same PAT that 403s on `git push` succeeds on every API call. Extracting to a standalone `monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js` lets both push sites (build.js publish, decider self-apply) share the implementation and avoids duplicating ~190 lines of API plumbing.

patches.rationale: New standalone Node script. Reads file contents from process.cwd(), POSTs blobs, fetches current HEAD + tree, builds new tree with base_tree, creates commit, PATCHes refs/heads/main. Handles 422 (non-fast-forward) with one retry on a fresh HEAD. Multi-file commits in one tree. PAT discovery: $PAT env first, then git remote URL extraction (same pattern as workspace-sync.md Step 1). Missing local files are treated as deletes (tree entry with sha:null) so the script handles delete-and-update commits cleanly.
patches.regression_risk: NEW FILE. Not invoked by any existing code path until patch A2 / A3 land. Standalone test command: `cd <clone> && node monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js --files README.md --message "test"` (touches README.md as a smoke test — operator should test in a throwaway branch first).

patches.anchor_line_gh: ~191 (function declarations zone, right after runTolerant) and ~391 (the push call)
patches.description: Insert pushWithFallback() helper function in the function-declarations zone (right after runTolerant), then replace the push call inside the publish branch.
patches.helper_insertion_note: The helper function (helper_function_content above) must be inserted as a sibling to run() and runTolerant() — anywhere in the function-declaration zone before the `// ── Main ──` line. Recommended placement: right after the runTolerant() function definition (after line 234 in current build.js).
patches.rationale: pushWithFallback() tries `git push origin main` first (preserves existing behavior on success). On failure, captures `git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD` to enumerate the committed-but-unpushed files, then invokes `node monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js` with those files and the last commit message. On API success, `git fetch origin main` refreshes the local refs so subsequent commands (the existing git-gc, syncToWorkspace) see the new SHA. On API failure, exits 1 the same way the original `run()` did — preserving the contract that publish exits non-zero when push fails.
patches.regression_risk: LOW. Happy path (git push succeeds) unchanged — same execSync call, same exit semantics. Failure path now does ONE extra thing (API fallback) before giving up. If the API call hangs (network), the existing execSync inherits stdio so output is visible and the operator can Ctrl-C. No new dependencies beyond node's built-in fetch (Node 18+, which is already required for the existing scripts).

patches.description: Insert API fallback between the existing `PUSH_OUT=$(git push ...)` line and the existing `if [ $PUSH_RC -ne 0 ]` rescue block. On API success, set PUSH_RC=0 so the rescue branch is skipped.
patches.old_string:
# Net change: one push attempt, then either success or rescue. No retry,
# no sleep, no split-push, no further diagnostics. If push 403s, that is
# a known transient and we fall through immediately to the rescue path.
PUSH_OUT=$(git push origin main 2>&1)
PUSH_RC=$?

echo "$PUSH_OUT"
if [ $PUSH_RC -ne 0 ]; then
patches.new_string:
# Net change: one push attempt, then either success or rescue. No retry,
# no sleep, no split-push, no further diagnostics. If push 403s, that is
# a known transient and we fall through immediately to the rescue path.
# PROP-050 amendment: between the push and the rescue, we now try the
# Git Data API path; on API success we set PUSH_RC=0 so the rescue branch
# below is skipped (the commit is already on origin/main). On API failure
# we fall through to the universal-pusher rescue as before.
PUSH_OUT=$(git push origin main 2>&1)
PUSH_RC=$?

# PROP-050 Design A: if `git push` fails, try the Git Data API path before
# falling through to universal-pusher rescue. The same PAT that 403s on
# `git push` succeeds on the REST API (proven 2026-05-20 commit 7126e0fd).
# When the API path succeeds, set PUSH_RC=0 so the rescue branch below is
# skipped — the commit is already on origin/main, no further work needed.
# When the API path also fails, leave PUSH_RC as-is and continue into the
# universal-pusher block (data files copied to FUSE, workspace-sync rescue
# within 1h). The fallback is bounded: one attempt, then universal-pusher.
if [ $PUSH_RC -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "git push failed — attempting Git Data API fallback (PROP-050)..."
  CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | paste -sd,)
  if [ -n "$CHANGED" ]; then
    LAST_MSG=$(git log -1 --pretty=%B)
    if node monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js --files "$CHANGED" --message "$LAST_MSG" 2>&1; then
      # Refresh local origin/main so the post-push verification + workspace-
      # sync calls below see the new SHA. Failure to fetch is non-fatal:
      # the commit is on the remote either way.
      git fetch origin main 2>&1 | tail -1
      PUSH_RC=0
      echo "Git Data API fallback succeeded. Push complete via REST API path."
    else
      echo "Git Data API fallback ALSO failed. Falling through to universal-pusher rescue."
    fi
  else
    echo "git push failed but no committed-but-unpushed files detected; skipping API fallback."
  fi
fi

echo "$PUSH_OUT"
if [ $PUSH_RC -ne 0 ]; then
patches.rationale: Three-tier push: (1) git push, (2) Git Data API fallback (NEW), (3) universal-pusher → workspace-sync rescue. The universal-pusher block stays as the safety net for cases where BOTH push paths fail. Setting PUSH_RC=0 on API success keeps the existing post-push status-clearing block intact (lines 446-468) — important because the API success is a real push, status should be cleared.
patches.regression_risk: LOW. The fallback only runs when the git push has already failed. The universal-pusher branch still runs whenever the API path also fails — so no rescue path is lost. The git fetch after API success is the only new mutation; it cannot regress because we already established the commit is on the remote.

patches.description: Add monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js to workspace-sync's NEVER_PUSH array (parallel to audit-rewrite.js).
patches.old_string:
# PROP-041 Phase 2 (2026-05-16): audit-rewrite.js is a runtime audit script
  # (source code), edited only via git. Not in monitor/prompts/ so the dynamic
  # rule does not cover it — must be listed explicitly here.
  'monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js'
patches.new_string:
# PROP-041 Phase 2 (2026-05-16): audit-rewrite.js is a runtime audit script
  # (source code), edited only via git. Not in monitor/prompts/ so the dynamic
  # rule does not cover it — must be listed explicitly here.
  'monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js'
  # PROP-050 (2026-05-20): push-via-api.js is the Git Data API fallback push
  # script invoked by build.js and decider self-apply when `git push` 403s.
  # Same source-code classification as audit-rewrite.js.
  'monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js'
patches.rationale: Source code under monitor/scripts/ must never round-trip from FUSE — matches audit-rewrite.js classification. Without this, a FUSE-side edit to push-via-api.js would silently propagate to git via workspace-sync's universal-pusher; with it, FUSE writes are skipped.
patches.regression_risk: NONE. Adding a path to NEVER_PUSH only restricts FUSE→git copies; it cannot break the script's normal use (script is invoked from the clone where it was checked out, not from FUSE).

patches.description: Add push-via-api.js to the CLAUDE.md File Ownership section, parallel to audit-rewrite.js.

patches.new_string:
- `monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js` — PROP-041 Phase 2 mechanical content-preservation pre-check script. Decider Step 1m step 4 calls it before pushing an RW to the priority queue. Git-owned source code (NEVER_PUSH in workspace-sync.md).
- `monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js` — PROP-050 Git Data API fallback push script. Invoked by `build.js` publish flow and decider self-apply when `git push` 403s. Bypasses git's HTTPS push code path while using the same PAT. Git-owned source code (NEVER_PUSH in workspace-sync.md).
patches.rationale: Documentation only. Keeps CLAUDE.md File Ownership accurate per tinker's "CLAUDE.md Accuracy" audit standing requirement.

phase_2_design.label: Design B — workspace-sync mtime-drift backstop for docs/index.html
phase_2_design.rationale: Even with Design A operating, drift can occur in edge cases: a decider commit that only touches build-scripts/generate-html.js but no data files (template fix) — the data files in FUSE end up older than docs/index.html, but the HTML was generated against the OLD template. Or: decider's commit succeeds via git push, but the syncToWorkspace step (build.js line 400) fails silently — FUSE's docs/index.html is stale. Design B is a backstop: every hourly workspace-sync cycle, compare FUSE data mtime vs FUSE docs/index.html mtime; if data is newer, regenerate and push. Once Design A is operating, this case becomes rare and Design B usually no-ops.
phase_2_design.approach_choice: b2 (use workspace-sync's existing clone, generate-html.js writes to clone/docs/index.html, normal git push). Workspace-sync's identity has been pushing successfully throughout the decider 403 streak (per operational notes in workspace-sync.md line 506: "32+ push-failures over 5 days, 100% workspace-sync push success"), so normal git push is the simplest path. Approach b1 (generate to /tmp, push via API) is unnecessary complexity — only adopt if workspace-sync's git push starts failing.
phase_2_design.runtime_dependencies: workspace-sync already runs `node build.js html` (current Step 3 line 482) so generate-html.js dependencies are confirmed available in workspace-sync's runtime. No new runtime requirements.

patches.anchor_line_gh: ~491-493 (end of existing Step 3 data-staged regen, before `git status --porcelain`)
patches.description: Insert mtime-drift backstop block immediately after the existing Step 3 data-staged regen. Idempotent: skips if Step 3 already staged docs/index.html.
patches.old_string:
manually run 'node build.js publish' from a clone to clear it."
  fi
fi

git status --porcelain
patches.new_string:
manually run 'node build.js publish' from a clone to clear it."
  fi
fi

# PROP-050 Design B: mtime-drift backstop for the data-staged regen above.
# When data files DIDN'T change this cycle (smart_copy found nothing newer)
# but FUSE's data files are STILL newer than FUSE's docs/index.html, the
# live site has drifted. Common cause: a prior `build.js publish` push
# failed; the universal-pusher rescue path handles data files but does NOT
# regenerate docs/index.html. Without this backstop, drift can persist for
# days until the next analyst write or operator-triggered rebuild — as
# happened 2026-05-17..2026-05-20 (WIN-070 caption math, 2+ day drift).
#
# This block is idempotent: if Step 3 already staged docs/index.html (data
# was staged this cycle), we skip; otherwise we regenerate from clone's
# current data files and stage. The regen is a pure deterministic function
# of the data, so running it when nothing changed is a diff-level no-op.
HTML_FUSE_MTIME=$(stat -c %Y "${WORKSPACE}/docs/index.html" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
DRIFT_FILES=""
for f in data/wins.json data/sections.json data/uncounted-failures.json data/predictions.json; do
  DATA_FUSE_MTIME=$(stat -c %Y "${WORKSPACE}/$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  if [ "$DATA_FUSE_MTIME" -gt "$HTML_FUSE_MTIME" ]; then
    DRIFT_FILES="${DRIFT_FILES}  ${f} (data mtime $(date -u -d @${DATA_FUSE_MTIME} +%FT%TZ) > html mtime $(date -u -d @${HTML_FUSE_MTIME} +%FT%TZ))\n"
  fi
done

if [ -n "$DRIFT_FILES" ]; then
  if git diff --cached --name-only -- docs/index.html | grep -q .; then
    echo "mtime-drift backstop: docs/index.html already regenerated this cycle (Step 3 staged it); skipping backstop."
  else
    echo "mtime-drift backstop: data newer than docs/index.html in FUSE — regenerating:"
    printf "%b" "$DRIFT_FILES"
    # Copy FUSE data into clone so the regen reflects the freshest available state.
    # Skipped if clone's data already matches FUSE (cmp short-circuits).
    for f in data/wins.json data/sections.json data/uncounted-failures.json data/predictions.json; do
      if [ -f "${WORKSPACE}/$f" ] && ! cmp -s "${WORKSPACE}/$f" "$f"; then
        cp "${WORKSPACE}/$f" "$f"
        git add "$f"
      fi
    done
    if node build.js html 2>&1 | tail -3; then
      # Sanity guard before staging: regenerated HTML must be non-trivially sized
      # and start with <!DOCTYPE html>. The current docs/index.html is ~3.6MB so
      # a <100KB regen is a generator bug. Refuse to stage corrupt output; the
      # integrity agent will surface the failure on its next daily run.
      REGEN_SIZE=$(stat -c %s docs/index.html 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
      if [ "$REGEN_SIZE" -lt 100000 ]; then
        echo "  WARN: regenerated docs/index.html is suspiciously small (${REGEN_SIZE} bytes). Refusing to stage."
      elif ! head -1 docs/index.html | grep -qF '<!DOCTYPE html>'; then
        echo "  WARN: regenerated docs/index.html missing <!DOCTYPE html> on line 1. Refusing to stage."
      else
        git add docs/index.html
        echo "  → Staged regenerated docs/index.html (md5 $(md5sum docs/index.html | cut -c1-8))."
      fi
    else
      echo "  WARN: 'node build.js html' failed during mtime-drift backstop. Continuing without regen."
    fi
  fi
fi

git status --porcelain
patches.rationale: New block runs after the existing data-staged regen and before `git status --porcelain`. The existing Step 4 (`git add -A monitor/ data/` on line 469 already ran; the new block does its own `git add` for any regenerated docs/index.html). Step 4 then commits everything together. The DOCS_NOTE in the existing commit message (lines 772-776) currently mentions "universal-pusher follow-up" only — the regen could happen from either path, but the existing language is broad enough; no commit message change required.
patches.regression_risk: LOW. The block is idempotent and self-contained: if data is NOT newer than HTML, the block is a no-op. If data IS newer, it copies FUSE→clone for the relevant data files (already done by smart_copy in Step 2 if smart_copy chose to copy, but defensive: cmp short-circuits when content is identical), regenerates HTML, sanity-checks, stages. The sanity guards (size > 100KB, starts with <!DOCTYPE html>) prevent pushing corrupted output. Worst case: regen fails → no push → integrity agent flags drift on its next daily run (status quo).

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: What if the Git Data API push ALSO fails (token expired mid-run, org permission removed)?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Three-tier fallback preserved. (1) `git push origin main` first. (2) API fallback (Design A). (3) Existing universal-pusher rescue: copy committed-but-unpushed files to FUSE; workspace-sync rescues via its own session within 1h. If ALL THREE fail consistently, that is a real outage — operator-side intervention required (PAT/org-auth diagnosis). Design A logs API failure details to push-failure-*.json (same path as today's push-failure logging in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md line 420-433) so operator has the diagnostic. Sequencing: API failure does NOT short-circuit the universal-pusher branch; PUSH_RC stays non-zero, the existing rescue runs as today.

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: What if there are merge conflicts — does the Git Data API path detect them?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Yes. The Git Data API's ref-update step (PATCH /git/refs/heads/main) uses `force: false`. If a concurrent commit landed between our fetch HEAD (step 2) and ref-update (step 6), the API returns 422 "Update is not a fast-forward". The script handles 422 with ONE retry on a fresh HEAD: re-fetch HEAD, re-build the tree using the NEW base_tree (which incorporates the intervening commit), re-create the commit, retry the PATCH. After one retry the script gives up — same discipline as `git push origin main` (one attempt after rebase, then human). The 422 retry is bounded to one cycle because if we hit 422 twice, there's sustained concurrent traffic and an agent should not loop indefinitely. Note: the Git Data API does NOT do content-level merge conflict resolution. If concurrent commits modified the SAME paths we're pushing, the second commit overwrites the first commit's changes to those paths. This is the same behavior as `git push --force-with-lease` — but we DO NOT use force. The 422 on non-fast-forward catches the most common case (concurrent commits on different paths still produce a fast-forward in git, so 422 only fires when our base_tree is genuinely stale).

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: What if A and B fire on the same revision — does workspace-sync's regen push step on top of decider's API fallback create a double-commit/race?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: No. Sequence: (1) Decider runs `node build.js publish`. (2) git push 403s, API fallback fires, commit lands on origin/main with both data and docs/index.html. (3) build.js syncToWorkspace step (line 400) copies the new docs/index.html and data files from clone → FUSE; both get the same mtime (fs.copyFileSync). (4) Workspace-sync runs hourly. At its next run, FUSE's docs/index.html mtime ≈ FUSE's data files mtime (both from the same publish-fallback). Design B's mtime check: `data_mtime > html_mtime`? FALSE (within the same second). No regen, no double push. If syncToWorkspace fails to copy docs/index.html to FUSE for some reason, FUSE's docs/index.html stays from a PREVIOUS publish (older mtime); then data IS newer; Design B regenerates from FUSE's CURRENT data files (which match the API fallback commit) and pushes. Net effect: one additional commit, but no race — the API fallback already settled origin/main, Design B's commit is just a reaffirmation of the same HTML content (since the regen is deterministic from the same data). If the diff is empty (content identical), Step 4's `git diff --cached --quiet` short-circuits and no empty commit is created.

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: What if regen produces a corrupted HTML (generator bug) — does workspace-sync have a sanity check before pushing?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Yes. Design B includes two pre-stage guards: (1) `stat -c %s docs/index.html` must be ≥ 100KB (current docs/index.html is ~3.6MB; a sub-100KB output is a generator failure). (2) `head -1 docs/index.html | grep -qF '<!DOCTYPE html>'` must succeed. Either guard failing means the regen output is suspect and we refuse to `git add docs/index.html` — the bad output is in the clone only, never committed, never pushed. The existing integrity agent (`structure-integrity.md`, daily ~9 AM) catches deeper structural issues (link breakage, tab consistency) on its next run. Optional follow-up: integrate `monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js` style validation but that is heavier and not required for this PROP. Worst case: bad regen → no stage → no push → integrity agent flags it tomorrow → human investigates (same as today).

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: Are there OTHER generated artifacts beyond docs/index.html that also drift (e.g., docs/llms.txt, sitemap.xml)?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: Partially. docs/llms.txt + docs/sitemap.xml + docs/robots.txt are committed by decider via the social-agent → decider pipeline (social writes drafts to monitor/social/drafts/, decider commits to docs/). They are NOT deterministic functions of data/, so Design B's data-mtime check does not apply. However, Design A handles them correctly: when decider commits these files, Design A pushes them via API if git push 403s — they're just files in `git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD`. docs/dome-review.pdf is OUT OF SCOPE for this PROP (operator preference; see out_of_scope section). If llms.txt / sitemap.xml / robots.txt specifically drift, the social agent's next daily run regenerates the drafts and the decider commits them — same loop as today, no additional gap.

counter_arguments_addressed.concern: Does workspace-sync's existing identity have the same SSO scope as Devilwench, or is it on a different PAT?
counter_arguments_addressed.answer: SAME PAT. Both decider and workspace-sync extract the PAT from `git -C "${WORKSPACE}" remote get-url origin` (workspace-sync.md line 19-20; decider inherits from its clone's remote URL set when the clone was created). The asymmetry is empirical — workspace-sync's session/IP has been pushing successfully throughout the decider 403 streak ("32+ push-failures over 5 days, 100% workspace-sync push success" per workspace-sync.md operational notes). The 403 is per-session/per-IP at GitHub's end (NOT a PAT scope problem — PAT contents:write is verified per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001). Design implication: Design A works NOT because it uses a different PAT, but because the REST API path bypasses git's HTTPS push protocol entirely, sidestepping whatever per-session/IP check is blocking the push. Design B works NOT because workspace-sync has a different PAT, but because workspace-sync's session/IP empirically has not been on the 403 list. If workspace-sync's session ALSO starts 403ing on git push, Design B can adopt approach b1 (push via API) as a follow-up — but that contingency is bounded and operator-controlled, not part of this PROP.

post_patch_behavior.existing_stranded_patches_clearance: Design A is FORWARD-LOOKING. Existing stranded-patches files (e.g., the 52h-old monitor/decisions/stranded-patches-2026-05-17T22-20-* per prior tinker summary) are NOT retroactively pushed by Design A. The operator's manual workflow remains: apply the stranded patch in an own-clone, push (which can now succeed via the API fallback if the operator invokes `node build.js publish` or pushes via API directly), then tombstone the sentinel per the PROP-016 Mech A convention. Tinker's Mode 1 stranded-patches detection (lines 128-153 of tinker.md) continues to age out un-tombstoned files >24h old as "needs operator attention."
post_patch_behavior.new_stranded_patches_after_landing: Rare. The 3-tier fallback (git push → API → universal-pusher) means a new stranded-patches file is only written if (a) git push fails AND (b) API fallback fails. The universal-pusher branch still triggers when both fail, and it does NOT write stranded-patches-*.json — that file is written by a separate code path (decider patches and selfapply, Mech A wrapper). The existing stranded-patches sentinel logic is not touched by this PROP.
post_patch_behavior.drift_recurrence_after_landing: Design A alone closes the HTML-drift gap for every commit that goes through `build.js publish` or decider self-apply. Design B closes any residual drift case (e.g., syncToWorkspace failure leaving FUSE behind). After both land, the 2-day drift incidents (today's incident, ISS-2095, the prior recurrences) should reduce to zero. Telemetry: count drift incidents in tinker reports going forward (drift = `node build.js publish` commit gap > 24h despite analyst/decider commits in window).

out_of_scope.pdf_regeneration: docs/dome-review.pdf is currently 2+ days stale per the directive's evidence section. PDF regeneration requires Playwright + headless Chromium (build-scripts/generate-pdf.js). Workspace-sync's runtime has Node but not necessarily Playwright; even if it did, headless Chromium adds significant runtime (~30-60s) and memory overhead. Per operator preference in DIRECTIVE-20260520-001.operator_preferences_for_proposal: "PDF regeneration is OUT OF SCOPE — Playwright is heavy, workspace-sync's environment doesn't have it. docs/dome-review.pdf staleness is a separate problem." PUNTED to a follow-on PROP if operator wants it.
out_of_scope.pat_rotation_or_org_sso_fix: Per directive: "PAT rotation has been attempted; the auth gap appears to be at the org SSO/identity layer (funwithscience-org), not the token itself. Tinker should treat 'fix the PAT' as outside the realistic option space and design around it." Design A and B both operate within the constraint that the PAT cannot be changed.
out_of_scope.build_js_publish_general_refactor: Per directive: "Do NOT redesign build.js publish flow generally — A is a surgical fallback, not a refactor." The patches keep the existing publish branch structure and only swap one `run()` call for a `pushWithFallback()` call plus a helper-function insertion.
out_of_scope.workspace_sync_b1_approach: Approach b1 (workspace-sync generates docs/index.html in /tmp and pushes via API) is documented as a contingency but NOT adopted in this PROP. Approach b2 (use existing clone + normal git push) is simpler and works because workspace-sync's git push has empirically been succeeding. Adopt b1 only if workspace-sync starts 403ing too.

regression_check.happy_path_unchanged: When `git push origin main` succeeds (the normal case for non-Devilwench sessions), Design A is a one-line addition (the try/catch wrapper) that exits the same way the existing `run()` did. No behavior change.
regression_check.fallback_path_isolated: API fallback only fires when git push has already failed. It cannot regress the happy path.
regression_check.universal_pusher_preserved: If BOTH git push AND API fallback fail, the existing universal-pusher rescue branch (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md lines 388-435) runs unchanged. No rescue path is removed.
regression_check.workspace_sync_step_3_idempotency: Design B's mtime backstop skips when Step 3 has already staged docs/index.html (Step 3 fires when data was staged this cycle). It only does additional work when Step 3 did NOT regenerate. No double-regen, no double-stage.
regression_check.sanity_guards: Design B refuses to stage corrupt regen output (<100KB or missing <!DOCTYPE html>). The clone's docs/index.html may be transient bad output but it is never committed and never pushed; the clone is rm -rf'd at run end (workspace-sync.md Cleanup section).
regression_check.pat_scope_unchanged: Both A and B use the same PAT and same `contents:write` scope. No new permissions required.
regression_check.no_force_push: Design A uses `force: false` on the ref-update PATCH. Design B uses normal `git push origin main`. No history rewriting, no force-pushes — same GIT SAFETY rules as existing code (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md lines 485-491).
test_plan_for_operator: STEP 1: In a throwaway branch on a clean clone, create monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js (patch PROP-050-A1). Verify standalone: `echo "test" > /tmp/smoke && node monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js --files /tmp/smoke --message "smoke" --branch <throwaway-branch>`.

STEP 2: Apply PROP-050-A2 (build.js helper). Run `node build.js publish` in a session that normally 403s. Expect: git push fails → API fallback triggers → publish completes → commit visible on origin/main.
STEP 3: Apply PROP-050-A3 (decider patches edit). Wait for a decider run that includes patches. Watch its push: expect the same fallback chain.
STEP 4: Apply PROP-050-A4 + PROP-050-A5 (NEVER_PUSH + CLAUDE.md docs). No runtime test needed — purely classification.

STEP 5: Apply PROP-050-B1 (workspace-sync mtime backstop). Wait for an hourly cycle. Verify: if FUSE docs/index.html is older than FUSE data files at that moment, backstop fires and regenerates. If not, backstop is a no-op.

ROLLBACK: each patch is independently reversible by git revert. A1+A2 can land without A3 (publish path only). B1 can land without A1-A5 (it just regenerates HTML; the regenerated HTML still pushes via workspace-sync's normal git push which has been working).

open_questions_for_operator: Should the API fallback be logged in monitor/integrity/push-failure-*.json (same schema as today's logs) so the operator has a paper trail of how often A fires? Default in this PROP: yes, log on both success-via-fallback and failure-via-fallback; can be silenced if too noisy.

Should Design B fire on ALL workspace-sync runs (every hour) or only when FUSE has been quiet for >N hours (e.g., signal that publish has been failing)? Default in this PROP: every run. The mtime check is cheap and idempotent; running it always is simpler than introducing a quiet-period threshold.

Should the API fallback also handle `git pull --rebase` for non-fast-forward cases? Currently the script handles 422 with one retry; if the retry also 422s the operator must intervene. We could extend to N retries with exponential backoff but that risks looping on a real outage. Default in this PROP: 1 retry, then exit.
fuse_staleness_footnote: This PROP was authored by tinker in a session that reads from FUSE for the workspace-sync.md / decider-patches-and-selfapply.md / build.js / CLAUDE.md source. Line numbers in the patches reference current FUSE state which should match origin/main given workspace-sync's recent activity. Operator applying these patches should `git pull --rebase` on their clone first to confirm line numbers still align; if upstream moved, the old_string anchors are unique enough to land correctly via Edit replace semantics.
integration_note: Applied via Git Data API single-commit push. Q1=silent (no monitor/integrity/push-failure-*.json logs — patches use console output only). Q2=every-run (default). Q3=1-retry-then-exit (default). All 6 patches landed: A1 new script, A2 build.js helper+swap, A3 decider md, A4 workspace-sync NEVER_PUSH, A5 CLAUDE.md, B1 workspace-sync Step 3 backstop. Verification grep: PROP-050 markers present in all 5 files. build.js parses (node -c). DIRECTIVE-20260520-001 already marked completed by tinker run that authored this PROP.
